thread.title: Schwarzenegger wore 'McFelon' T-shirt mocking Trump? No, image is altered | Fact check
thread.title_full: Schwarzenegger wore 'McFelon' T-shirt mocking Trump? No, image is altered | Fact check

author: , Norwich Bulletin
title: Schwarzenegger wore 'McFelon' T-shirt mocking Trump? No, image is altered | Fact check
text:
Schwarzenegger wore 'McFelon' T-shirt mocking Trump? No, image is altered | Fact check
The claim: Image shows Arnold Schwarzenegger wearing anti-Trump T-shirt
A Nov. 19 Threads post ([direct link](https://www.threads.net/@m_sh2o/post/DCjiiyXunH4), [archive link](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/25379159-112224-threads-arnold-1)) appears to show actor and former California Gov. Arnold Schwarzenegger wearing a T-shirt mocking President-elect Donald Trump.
“I didn’t vote for the McFelon," reads the message on the shirt, which also shows the McDonald’s golden arches logo beneath a graphic of Trump’s trademark hairstyle.
The post received more than 100 likes in three days. A similar post on Threads received [thousands of likes](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/25379160-112224-threads-arnold-2) before it was deleted. The image was also shared [thousands of times on X, formerly Twitter](https://mvau.lt/media/07544861-9faf-429d-8f5c-ca2619c5a0e7).
More from the Fact-Check Team: [How we pick and research claims](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/factcheck/2020/02/12/fact-check-guidelines-usa-today/4735217002/) | [Email newsletter](https://profile.usatoday.com/newsletters/checking-the-facts/) | [Facebook page](https://www.facebook.com/usatodayfactcheck/)
Our rating: Altered
The image was digitally edited to add the anti-Trump message on Schwarzenegger’s shirt.
Schwarzenegger's shirt blank in original image
Schwarzenegger, a Republican, served as [California's governor from 2003 to 2011](https://www.nga.org/governor/arnold-schwarzenegger/) but [supported Democratic Vice President Kamala Harris’ bid](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/30/arnold-schwarzenegger-endorses-kamala-harris-and-tim-walz/75939473007/) for president in the 2024 election.
He did not, however, pose while wearing a T-shirt with an anti-Trump slogan. The image in the Threads post was digitally altered to make it appear as if he did.
Fact check: ['Hitler Did Nothing Wrong' message edited onto Laura Loomer's photo](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/30/arnold-schwarzenegger-endorses-kamala-harris-and-tim-walz/75939473007/)
[Schwarzenegger posted](https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/25379184-112224-instagram-arnold-original-photo) the original image [to Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/p/C4kpERDoJ6a/?img_index=9) on March 16. It shows him wearing a blank T-shirt and smiling as he poses alongside fellow bodybuilder Phil Heath in a gym. The background and the expression on the former governor’s face match those in the version shared to Threads. Heath is cropped out of the altered version of the image.
The slogan edited onto the T-shirt references Trump’s [much-publicized love](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/trump/2024/11/16/jfk-jr-criticizes-trumps-love-of-fast-food-mcdonalds-diet-coke/76288527007/#:~:text=Then%20there%E2%80%99s%20the%20fast-food%20affection.%20Any%20fast%20food:%20burgers,%20fried%20chicken,%20pizza.) [of fast food](https://www.instagram.com/p/BF4raEHmhag/?utm_source=ig_embed) along with his [conviction on 34 counts of falsifying business records](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2024/05/30/donald-trump-verdict-criminal-hush-money-trial/73891042007/) to conceal a hush money payment to an adult film actress before the 2016 presidential election. His sentencing in that case, most recently [scheduled for Nov. 26](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/11/19/trump-sentencing-hush-money-case-adjourned/76190286007/), [was postponed indefinitely](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/11/22/trump-new-york-hush-money-sentencing-postponed/76497161007/) on Nov. 22.
[Schwarzenegger gave Harris](https://www.elpasotimes.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/30/arnold-schwarzenegger-endorses-kamala-harris-and-tim-walz/75939473007/) and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, [his endorsement](https://x.com/Schwarzenegger/status/1851627802027758005) in the presidential race, writing in an Oct. 30 X post "I will always be an American before I am a Republican."
USA TODAY previously debunked similar images of political messages being edited onto clothing worn by public figures including [musicians Taylor Swift](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/factcheck/2024/01/22/altered-image-shows-taylor-swift-in-anti-trump-t-shirt-fact-check/72311842007/) and [Kid Rock](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/factcheck/2024/01/31/kid-rock-joe-hunter-biden-t-shirt-fact-check/72422055007/) and [former First Lady Michelle Obama](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/factcheck/2024/04/29/donald-trump-shirt-michelle-obama/73500001007/).
USA TODAY reached out to multiple social media users who shared the image but did not immediately receive any responses.
[Check Your Fact](https://checkyourfact.com/2024/11/21/fact-check-arnold-schwarzenegger-wear-shirt-i-didnt-vote-for-the-mcfelon/) also debunked the claim.
Our fact-check sources
- Arnold Schwarzenegger, March 16,
[Instagram post](https://www.instagram.com/p/C4kpERDoJ6a/?img_index=9)

USA TODAY is a [verified signatory](https://ifcncodeofprinciples.poynter.org/profile/usa-today) of the International Fact-Checking Network, which requires a demonstrated commitment to nonpartisanship, fairness and transparency.
